Model Pipeline v7

✅ Step 1 — 4.2M sales, 2004–2025
✅ Step 2 — Strata classification (house/unit)
✅ Step 3 — Hedonic XGBoost — separate house & unit
✅ Step 4 — LightGBM Suburb — separate house & unit
✅ Step 5 — Monte Carlo GBM — separate house & unit
✅ Step 6 — School quality (IEO/SEIFA) as feature
✅ Step 7 — Interactive Dashboard v7

Quality Filters

🏠 Houses: sale ≥ $300K · suburb median ≥ $500K · ≥5 sales/qtr
🏢 Units: sale ≥ $200K · suburb median ≥ $300K · ≥5 sales/qtr
📊 Classification: strata_lot_number field (reliable, 82,888 units)
🎓 School quality: IEO (Index of Education & Opportunity)
📈 Result: House suburb R² 0.66→0.89, MAPE 13.75%→10.8%

Accuracy Summary

🏠 House Models

🏢 Unit Models

Compare up to 4 suburbs — historical prices, 12-month forecast, and 20-year projection. The House/Unit toggle in the header controls which property type's forecast and long-term data is shown. The 🏠 vs 🏢 Split tab shows both house and unit price history on the same chart regardless of the toggle.
⚠️ Some suburbs only have house or unit history (e.g. Homebush West has unit data but not enough house sales for a type-specific forecast). This reflects real market composition, not a data error.

Historical Median Prices

Suburb Statistics

Hedonic XGBoost v5 — separate models for houses and units. Log-transform target, early stopping, 600 estimators. House model uses land area features; unit model emphasises location (distance to CBD, SEIFA/IEO). IEO = Index of Education & Opportunity (ABS SEIFA 2021) — proxy for school zone quality, 98.3% suburb coverage.

🏠 House Hedonic Model

🏢 Unit Hedonic Model

⚠️ Unit model R²=0.243 reflects genuine complexity: internal floor area, floor level, building age, parking, and view quality are not in the public dataset. The street-address + suburb median features capture ~24% of unit price variation. The IEO/school-quality feature was the 4th most important feature in both models.

House vs Unit — Key Differences

🏠 House model top features:
  1. rolling_8q_median (suburb trend)
  2. days_since_2000 (time trend)
  3. rolling_4q_median
  4. ieo_score (school quality)
  5. suburb_price_index
  6. log_area (land area)
🏢 Unit model top features:
  1. rolling_8q_median (suburb trend)
  2. days_since_2000 (time trend)
  3. dist_cbd_km (location premium)
  4. ieo_score (school quality)
  5. irsad_score (socio-economic)
  6. postcode_int

🏠 Showing House Long-Term Projections — 2,212 suburbs · MC GBM v5 · 2,000 paths
Monte Carlo GBM v5 — 2,000 paths per suburb. Drift and volatility calibrated from 20yr YoY log-returns on 8-quarter type-specific rolling medians. Drift bounded [-2%, +12%], vol [3%, 18%], 70%/30% long-run/recent blend. Drag sliders to explore scenarios in real time.

Simplified Price Estimator — uses the type-specific suburb median and property characteristics to produce a range estimate. The Property Type field automatically uses the matching house or unit forecast data. This is a heuristic approximation (±25–35%). Not financial advice.
